cit.basis: Flat 10% corporate income tax (Corporate Income Tax Act / ZKPO) on taxable profit; no progressive brackets.
cit.pillar_two_note: Despite the 10% headline CIT rate, Bulgaria implemented EU Pillar Two (Directive (EU) 2022/2523) as of 2024, including a Qualified Domestic Minimum Top-up Tax (QDMTT) bringing in-scope groups (consolidated revenue >= EUR 750m) to a 15% effective minimum. Out of scope for this SME estimator.
cit.note: Administered by the National Revenue Agency (NRA / NAP).

vat.note: 20% standard VAT (DDS); 9% reduced rate for hotel/tourist accommodation. EU member state: One-Stop-Shop (OSS/IOSS) available for cross-border B2C supplies.

personal_income_tax.note: Flat 10% personal income tax on employment and most other income.

currency_transition.note: Bulgaria adopts the euro on 1 January 2026; the 2025 target year still uses the Bulgarian lev (BGN), pegged at 1.95583 BGN/EUR under the currency board.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Pillar Two/QDMTT, one-off alternative taxes, withholding, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
